words.definition: To make a process operate automatically by using machines or computers.
words.example: Many manufacturing processes have been automated to increase efficiency and reduce human error.

words.definition: A set of rules or steps to be followed in calculations or other problem-solving operations, especially by a computer.
words.example: Social media platforms use complex algorithms to determine which content appears on a user's feed.

words.definition: Causing radical change in an industry or market, typically by introducing new technology.
words.example: Artificial intelligence is considered a disruptive technology that will reshape the global job market.

words.definition: No longer produced or used; out of date.
words.example: Rapid technological advancement means that hardware can become obsolete within just a few years.

words.definition: To make something greater by adding to it; in tech, to enhance human capabilities with technology.
words.example: Augmented reality is used to augment the user's perception of the physical world.

words.definition: Relating to moral principles; often used in AI discussions regarding bias and privacy.
words.example: The development of autonomous vehicles raises significant ethical concerns regarding liability in accidents.
Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern society, changing how we communicate.

words.definition: Susceptible to physical or emotional attack or damage; in tech, susceptible to security breaches.
words.example: Without proper encryption, personal data remains vulnerable to cyber-attacks.
